title: READABILITY: Document connection_seq and MLS admin election invariant
description: relay/src/ws.rs:193-201 — The code sorts online users by connection_seq and stamps each entry with its sequence number. The comment mentions 'lowest sequence = admin' but doesn't explain why this matters or what the load-bearing invariant is. Per CLAUDE.md MLS Admin Election Invariant section, online[0] is the MLS group admin because the relay assigns monotonic sequence values and clients sort by them. This ensures all peers independently elect the same admin. A reader needs to understand this is not just sorting; it's a coordinated leader election. Fix: expand the comment to mention this is for MLS admin election and cross-reference or inline the key invariant: 'online[0] is always the admin because all peers sort by this relay-stamped sequence value.'
close_reason: P4 readability/comment items. Not blocking correctness. Deferred — file separately if prioritized.
